candidates.name: Balanced Talos + LLM Tower
candidates.summary: A phased AM5 tower that joins a Talos cluster now and adds stronger local inference later.
candidates.binding_constraint: budget plus future NVIDIA upgrade path

phases.goal: Deploy a useful Talos worker immediately

parts.rationale: Efficient 8-core CPU with enough headroom for Talos workloads and CPU-only inference.
parts.why_not_alternatives: 7600 leaves less headroom; 7900 adds cost with limited benefit for this use case.

parts.rationale: AM5 board with solid VRM and room for a later GPU plus storage expansion.
parts.why_not_alternatives: Comparable board, but this SKU had better AU availability.

parts.rationale: Enough memory for Talos workloads today and CPU-side model headroom.
parts.why_not_alternatives: 32GB is the tighter long-term limit for mixed workloads.

parts.rationale: Room for OS, images, and local model storage without overpaying for Gen5.
parts.why_not_alternatives: 1TB fills too quickly once models are added.

phases.delivered_capability: Talos worker with enough local storage and RAM for general cluster workloads and smaller quantized models.

parts.rationale: Best value path to medium-model local inference with CUDA support.
parts.why_not_alternatives: 4060 Ti lacks VRAM headroom; 4090 breaks the budget.

parts.rationale: Enough headroom for the later GPU without oversizing excessively.
parts.why_not_alternatives: RM750e is tighter once the 3090 is added.

phases.delivered_capability: Talos-compatible worker with practical CUDA-based medium-model inference.

candidates.tradeoffs: Phase 1 is intentionally CPU-only, so local inference is slower until the GPU is added.

recommendation.why: It preserves Talos compatibility, stays inside the phased budget, and leaves a clean upgrade path to NVIDIA inference.
recommendation.confidence_notes: Recommendation assumes the user is comfortable with a used 3090 in Phase 2.
